body_txt: ...'CAUSE THE FACE AIN'T LISTENING!!! So, you've got a guy who thinks fair fights are for suckers , and he's up against some idiot who won't shut up because he's trying to sound all cool and intimidating. Or perhaps he was smart enough to trick the foe into talking too much . The only thing for our Street Smart fighter to do in this case is— *WHAM* Naturally the opposite to Talking Is a Free Action ; many shows where the characters call their attacks have at least one instance where this trope overrides the normal approach . Great way to deliver a Shut Up, Hannibal! Offhand Backhand is a variant of this for extra cool points. Note that this can be used in an unsympathetic light, usually by a stupid and uninformed person (like, say, a Knight Templar or The Fundamentalist ) who has been backed into an ideological corner by someone who challenged their idiotic, benighted ramblings and defeated all of their counterarguments with an effective Kirk Summation ; if they really don't want to accept that they have nothing left to stand on, expect the stupid-and-angry character to punch them square in the face, often along with a terse "no one cares", or "shut the fuck up". It's also a great way to establish an unsympathetic character as a violent, thuggish scumbag for all of the same reasons. Because this kind of attack takes advantage of the victim not expecting to be hit, it is often seen as a particularly dirty and cowardly attack; for this reason, sucker punches are called 'coward punches' in several regions of the Commonwealth, particularly Australia. In a worst case scenario, it can kill the recipient, usually from them falling and striking their skull on hard objects. If the attack is fatal, this can overlap with Killed Mid-Sentence . Can also overlap with the Fisticuff-Provoking Comment which is similar but timing-insensitive and not about interrupting someone else while they're talking. Sister trope of [Verb] This! . Not to be confused with Talk to the Hand .
